cap.status_board.name: Status Board
cap.status_board.desc: Tier 0 escape-code status board (agents emit state; iTerm2 paints it).
cap.worktree_isolation.name: Worktree Isolation
cap.messaging.name: Messaging
cap.messaging.desc: Cross-tab agent-to-agent messaging via the broker.
cap.agent_inbox.name: Agent Inbox
cap.cost_dashboard.name: Cost Dashboard
cap.janitor.name: Janitor
cap.mcp.name: MCP
cap.mcp.desc: MCP surface exposing spawnterm to agents.
cap.daemon.name: Daemon
cap.daemon.desc: Tier 1 iTerm2 Python API orchestration daemon (registry + ingest/idle).
cap.broker.name: Broker
cap.broker.desc: Tier 2 external broker (durable sqlite mailbox/registry/state/ack over a unix socket).
cap.review.name: Review
cap.review.desc: Per-agent diff/review surface (show worktree diff vs base; approve->merge / request-changes).
cap.tmux.name: tmux Persistence
cap.tmux.desc: Tier 3 tmux -CC persistence: spawn agents inside a native tmux -CC session so windows/agents survive quit/crash and can be reattached.
cap.claude_statusbar.name: Claude Status Bar
cap.claude_statusbar.desc: Claude Code session status aggregator status-bar component (Waiting/Working/Idle across all windows).
cap.agent_menubar.name: Agent Menu Bar
cap.agent_menubar.desc: Menu bar status item showing a live count badge of busy AI agents (imported from gnachman/iTerm2#670).
cap.codex_status.name: Codex Status
cap.codex_status.desc: Show Codex CLI working/idle activity in the tab status by decoding the braille-spinner title prefix (imported from gnachman/iTerm2#673).
help.intro: Print the capability guide, then a live list of currently enabled feature flags.
help.no_flags: No feature flags are enabled (all default OFF).
gate.off: spawnterm.{0} is off. Enable it with: spawnterm-flag enable spawnterm.{0}
